methodsAttempted.query: site:mines.edu Colorado School of Mines generative AI policy syllabus academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned the official Academic Affairs generative AI guidelines page, an official downloadable guidelines PDF, course PDFs, and catalog pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:mines.edu "Artificial Intelligence" "Colorado School of Mines" "Academic Integrity"
methodsAttempted.notes: Official-domain search again identified the Academic Affairs GenAI page and a Faculty Handbook research-integrity policy page that lists the GenAI guidelines as a related resource.
Robots.txt permits the public GenAI page and disallows /wp-content/uploads/; the public HTML page was fetched, while the linked PDF was not used as evidence.
The official catalog research-integrity page was fetchable and official but broader than GenAI policy, so it was rejected as primary AI-policy evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a central Colorado School of Mines Academic Affairs page for generative AI guidelines. The linked PDF was not used because the source host disallows /wp-content/uploads/ in robots.txt, and the catalog research-integrity page was treated as too broad for primary GenAI claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Using Generative Artificial Intelligence at Mines

artifacts.claimText: Colorado School of Mines Academic Affairs maintains official generative-AI guidance, but the page frames the material as guidelines rather than a universal policy prescribing when GenAI must be allowed or disallowed.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Using Generative Artificial Intelligence at Mines
citation.publisher: Colorado School of Mines Academic Affairs
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: In the absence of contrary written instructor directions, Mines guidance says students may not submit evaluation work generated in whole or in part by GenAI, while GenAI use for learning, studying, proofreading, and brainstorming is permitted and encouraged unless instructors state otherwise.

artifacts.claimText: Mines sample syllabus language says students who include AI-generated ideas, text, code, or images in submitted work must document and credit the source, and that failure to properly cite AI tools would be considered academic misconduct.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Using Generative Artificial Intelligence at Mines
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unless instructors provide explicit permission or instruction to the contrary, students may not submit content for evaluation that was generated, in whole or in part, by genAI tools. Using genAI tools when unauthorized would be considered a potential violation of Mines' policy on academic integrity.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status claim because the wording preserves the page's limitation that it is guidance, not a universal allow/disallow policy.
Approved as a narrow default-guidance claim tied to the page's Default Guidelines section and conditioned on absence of contrary written instructor directions.
Approved as a sample-syllabus and academic-integrity claim; it does not infer a separate misconduct procedure beyond the linked Mines policy reference.
Approved as teaching guidance because the evidence directly addresses written course expectations for GenAI use.

artifacts.title: Colorado School of Mines GenAI Guidance Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Colorado School of Mines has an official Academic Affairs page with generative-AI guidelines for academic work. The staged claims are limited to the guidance page's own framing, default student-use guidance, citation and academic-integrity language, and faculty communication expectations.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The source page expressly says it does not prescribe universal policies for when to allow or disallow generative AI.
The linked official PDF was not used as evidence because robots.txt disallows the uploads path.
